steps.title: Set up the equation for an 8% increase
steps.content: When n is increased by 8%, the new value is $1.08n$. We're told this equals 432: $$1.08n = 432$$

steps.title: Test choice C
steps.content: Backsolving - testing choice C (400): If $n = 400$, then $$1.08 \times 400 = 400 + 0.08 \times 400 = 400 + 32 = 432$$ That confirms C is correct.
